new_classes.label_de: Gin, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l (KN 2208 50 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin produced by distilling rectified grain spirit or ethyl alcohol using juniper berries and other aromatic substances, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres. Classified under CN code 2208 50 19.

coverage_explanation: The legal text describes gin (22085011 and 22085019) as an alcoholic beverage generally produced from rectified grain spirit or ethyl alcohol by simple or successive distillation using juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise). The key discriminating criterion between 22085011 (≤ 2 l) and 22085019 (> 2 l) is the container size. The proposed axioms capture: (1) gin spirituous quality, (2) juniper berry aromatic quality, (3) production by gin distillation process, and (4) container size > 2 l (via LargeContainer). A disjointness complement with GinSmallContainer22085011 is also added. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the legal text also references aromatic substances quality (AromaticSubstanceFlavouring) more broadly, and the base grain/ethyl alcohol origin is not formally encoded via a dedicated quality class in the current TBox. The text is also truncated so some detail may be missing.
